_doc: Compiled from functional_pathways.txt — Pathway interpretation algorithm

unweighted_vs_weighted.approximately_equal: Pathways are complete and stable — category functioning reliably
unweighted_vs_weighted.unweighted_much_higher: Pathways detected but fragmented — coverage gaps limit efficiency
unweighted_vs_weighted.both_below_0.0005: Metabolic category is minimal — functionally marginal
unweighted_vs_weighted.both_above_0.0015: Strong metabolic capacity — important functional contributor

high.label: High redundancy
high.meaning: Multiple carriers per pathway; robust. Loss of one has minimal impact.

moderate.label: Moderate redundancy
moderate.meaning: Most pathways have backup; some dominated. Selective loss can affect flow.

low.label: Low redundancy
low.meaning: Many pathways rely on 1-2 carriers. Fragile.

very_low.label: Very low redundancy
very_low.meaning: Single-carrier pathways dominate. Loss = functional collapse.

strong_stable.client_frame: Your microbiome reliably produces [function]. This is a strength.

working_at_risk.client_frame: Your microbiome produces [function], but a few key species carry most of the load. Supporting these species would strengthen resilience.

severely_limited.client_frame: This metabolic capacity is at risk and may need targeted intervention.

tier_1_cofactor: B vitamins (B1, B2, B5, B6, B7, B9, B12, K)

bottleneck_scoring.severe: ≥2 bottlenecks in Tier 0 — cascading effects likely
bottleneck_scoring.high: ≥2 bottlenecks in Tier 1 — cofactor deficiency limits enzyme function

key_pathways.PWY-7111: Pyruvate fermentation to isobutanol — alternative SCFA, not protective butyrate
key_pathways.PWY-5676: Classical butyrate pathway (acetyl-CoA) — the protective pathway
key_pathways.PWY-6583: Pyruvate to butanoate — secondary butyrate pathway

quality_control.concern: F. prausnitzii/Roseburia detected but PWY-5676 absent
quality_control.likely_cause: Incomplete reference database, low sequencing depth, or HUMAnN annotation limitations
quality_control.guidance: Assume butyrate capacity present based on taxonomic detection. Confirm with fecal SCFA measurement if clinical concern.
